claims.claim: The capitalized cost to develop one approved new drug is about $2.6 billion (2013 dollars), per the DiMasi/Tufts CSDD estimate.
New drugs approved per inflation-adjusted billion dollars of R&D halved roughly every nine years from 1950, an ~80-fold decline dubbed Eroom's Law.
Across drug programs entering Phase 1, only about 13.8% eventually reach approval, with Phase 2 the lowest-success transition.
The Recursion-Roche/Genentech collaboration was announced at up to $12 billion but has paid Recursion only $213 million in upfront and milestone payments to date.
Isomorphic Labs' 2024 Eli Lilly collaboration carried a $45 million upfront payment against up to $1.7 billion in milestones.
Insilico Medicine's rentosertib, a generative-AI-discovered TNIK inhibitor, improved FVC by +98.4 mL in the 60 mg arm versus -20.3 mL on placebo over 12 weeks in a randomized Phase 2a IPF trial.
AI-discovered molecules show roughly 80-90% Phase 1 success but about 40% Phase 2 success, in line with historic industry averages.
The AlphaFold Protein Structure Database holds over 200 million predicted structures and the AlphaFold2 paper is among the most-cited papers in modern science.
Baker-lab RFdiffusion raised experimental binder-design success rates by about two orders of magnitude, but an independent crowdsourced EGFR competition found only a 14% wet-lab hit rate.
Eli Lilly deployed a wholly-owned NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with 1,016 Blackwell Ultra GPUs as a pharma AI factory.
Moderna deployed ChatGPT Enterprise to roughly 3,000 employees who built more than 750 custom GPTs across legal, research, manufacturing and commercial functions.
A Merck and McKinsey generative-AI tool cut clinical study report first-draft time from about 180 hours to 80 hours and reduced errors by 50%.
The FDA's January 2025 draft guidance on AI in drug development cited agency experience with more than 500 drug and biological product submissions containing AI components since 2016.
The FDA's AI-enabled medical device list exceeds 1,000 marketing authorizations, of which roughly 76% are radiology devices, yet only about 30% of US radiologists report using AI clinically.
Tempus AI grew revenue to about $1.3 billion in FY2025, up 83.4% year over year, while remaining net-loss-making.
Recursion acquired Exscientia in an all-stock merger (closed November 2024) leaving Recursion holders ~74% and Exscientia holders ~26% of the combined company.
A RAND controlled red-team study found no statistically significant uplift from current LLMs for biological-attack planning.

Chinese biopharma out-licensing reached a record of roughly $135.7 billion across 157 deals in 2025, about 2.6x the 2024 total, though most assets are conventional chemistry rather than AI-discovered.
